codeReview.summary: Pass. The prior blocker is resolved at current HEAD: `SandboxAgentBuilder::capabilities()` now normalizes duplicate capability entries before they can reach runtime tool resolution, `build()` repeats that normalization defensively, and the new regression proves subset-configured runs no longer produce duplicate fixed-name sandbox tools while omitted capabilities remain unavailable.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed review and synthesis, feature metadata, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, `rust-sandbox-worker`, current sandbox implementation, and commit history through `7f1b3d7b55f8b4ce369efdce35d5aeb5ecf8adb4`. Status: pass. `crates/agents-core/src/sandbox/mod.rs:52-56` adds set-style capability normalization, `crates/agents-core/src/sandbox/mod.rs:133-143` applies it on builder input and again during `build()`, and the subset-specific tool attachment path in `crates/agents-core/src/sandbox/mod.rs:616-674` therefore no longer emits duplicate fixed-name tools. The existing subset regression at `crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:288` still shows omitted capabilities stay absent, and the new duplicate-entry regression at `crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:381` verifies duplicate filesystem/apply_patch inputs yield a unique tool bundle, unique capability instructions, and successful runtime execution, satisfying VAL-SANDBOX-017.
